workDetail.label: Medidata Solutions
bullets: Own an enterprise data catalog platform used by 1,400+ customers — the metadata layer for a flagship data product
Championed replacing a homegrown catalog with a SaaS platform; built exec business case unlocking AI use cases
Defined metadata schemas & hierarchies enabling a new customer-facing data product marketplace
Manage agile roadmap & success metrics via Aha, Jira, Pendo, SQL

workDetail.label: Codifyd
bullets: Led business analysis for the MDM consulting practice across all client workstreams
Managed & mentored a team of 10 business analysts
Retired 10 legacy systems for a global food service provider — reduced analytics request time 35%+
Reduced new item setup ~25% for a building materials manufacturer via modernized EU regulatory data model
Acquired by Pivotree

workDetail.label: Scholastic
bullets: Launched global enterprise product catalog across e-commerce and downstream internal systems
Retired 20+ legacy data systems — unified customer experience, cut support tickets 25%
Managed 20+ senior stakeholders and 1,000+ internal end users

bullets: Business analysis of legacy systems to map future-state processes for a new master data platform
Tested and validated end-to-end integrations across supply chain, manufacturing, and planning platforms

running:Start.title: Start · Verrazzano Bridge
bullets: 11 NYC Marathon finishes — every year since 2014 (except 2020)
One of ~50,000 runners crossing the Verrazzano each November

running:Mile 13.title: Mile 13 · Pulaski Bridge
bullets: Halfway. Brooklyn behind you, Queens ahead.
The Pulaski is always louder than you expect.

running:Mile 16.title: Mile 16 · Queensboro Bridge
bullets: The bridge is silent — no spectators, just the sound of 50,000 feet.
You come off onto 1st Ave to the loudest crowd of the day.

running:Mile 21.title: Mile 21 · The Bronx
running:Mile 21.sub: The wall is real up here
bullets: The Bronx is short — about a mile — but it feels long at mile 21.
5 more miles back down through Harlem into Central Park.

running:Finish.title: Finish · Central Park
bullets: 11 finishes and counting.
Best time: check the results table.

bullets: Static site on GitHub Pages — no build step
Mapbox GL JS · MTA GTFS · GPX route data
Marathon results auto-fetched weekly via GitHub Actions

about.bio: I'm a Technical Product Manager based in NYC. I build data products, run the NYC Marathon every year, make ceramics and prints, and wrote a subway app because the MTA deserved better.
details: 💼 Data Platform Product Manager · 10 years experience

work.title: Work
work.body: 10 years building data products — enterprise catalogs, MDM platforms, and the integrations that make them useful. I've retired 30+ legacy systems, managed 1,000+ end users, and led teams from roadmap to release. Currently owning a data catalog platform at Medidata used by 1,400+ enterprise customers.
work.cta: Tap a stop on the orange line to see each role.

art.title: Art & Making
art.body: Wheel-thrown stoneware, hand-built vessels, screen printing, and linocut reduction prints in limited editions of 10–20. The clay doesn't negotiate — best counterweight to the product sprint.
art.cta: Tap a stop on the blue line to explore.

tech.title: Projects
tech.body: I built 1train.nyc — a real-time arrivals app for the NYC subway — because the MTA deserved better tooling. A PM who can ship. Flask, HTMX, PostgreSQL, GTFS-RT feeds, Python. This site is also hand-built: Mapbox GL JS, no framework, no build step.
tech.cta: Tap a stop on the green line to explore.

art.title: Art & Making
art.html:

The clay doesn't negotiate — best counterweight to the product sprint.
